factors.summary: The company is in a restructuring mode, with layoffs and workforce reductions to manage costs.

factors.summary: Labor relations are stable, though challenges exist due to constraints in labor availability.

evidence.text: The COVID-19 outbreak and associated counteracting measures implemented by governments and businesses around the world, as well as subsequent accelerated recovery in global business activity, have increased uncertainty in the global business environment and led to supply chain disruptions and shortages in global markets for commodities, logistics and labor, as well as input cost inflation.

factors.summary: Talent retention is a challenge due to labor constraints and supply chain issues.

evidence.text: The COVID-19 outbreak and associated counteracting measures implemented by governments and businesses around the world, as well as subsequent accelerated recovery in global business activity, have increased uncertainty in the global business environment and led to supply chain disruptions and shortages in global markets for commodities, logistics and labor, as well as input cost inflation.

factors.summary: R&D investment is stable, with expenses slightly decreasing as a percentage of revenue.

evidence.text: Research and development costs, including qualifying engineering costs, are expensed when incurred and amounted to $40.7 million and $41.2 million for the three months ended March 31, 2022 and 2021, respectively. These costs as a percent of revenue were 2.0% and 2.2% for the three months ended March 31, 2022 and 2021, respectively.

factors.summary: The company has demonstrated strong pricing power, successfully raising prices which have favorably impacted revenue across different quarters.

evidence.text: Customer pricing favorably impacted revenue by approximately 1.9% in the first quarter.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter 2022 Compared to the First Quarter 2021

evidence.text: Customer pricing favorably impacted revenue by approximately 6.0% in the first quarter of 2022. Pricing was higher than in prior quarters reflecting actions to recover increasing costs.

factors.summary: Backlog orders are growing significantly across multiple segments, reflecting strong demand. The evidence indicates a 22.6% year-over-year increase and a total backlog of $3.4 billion as of March 31, 2022, up from $2.2 billion the previous year.

evidence.text: Backlog increased 22.6% as compared to the prior year quarter.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter 2022 Compared to the First Quarter 2021

evidence.text: Backlog as of March 31, 2022 was $3.4 billion, an increase from $2.2 billion in the prior year. See definition of bookings and backlog within 'Segment Results of Operations'.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed, with some evidence of growth and challenges across different segments. While there are signs of strong demand in certain areas, other segments are facing difficulties such as component shortages and reduced activity.

evidence.text: Demand appears to be mixed across segments, with some evidence of growth in certain areas but challenges in others.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter 2022 Compared to the First Quarter 2021

evidence.text: Bookings were $2.3 billion for the three months ended March 31, 2022, a decrease of $80.4 million, or 3.4% compared to the prior year comparable period.
The bookings decline was driven primarily by a difficult comparable quarter in the prior year.
Book-to-bill was above 1.00 for all five segments in the quarter, a reflection of strong demand conditions and order intake in most end markets.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in serialization software, Engineered Products, Pumps & Process Solutions, and Climate & Sustainability Technologies, which is beneficial. However, challenges such as regional lockdowns in Asia and supply chain constraints have impacted revenue in certain areas.

evidence.text: The decline in revenue was further impacted as areas within Asia experienced regional lockdowns due to COVID-19 in March. We expect deliveries in our marking and coding business to improve as the year progresses as component shortages dissipate and underlying demand for our printers and consumables remains strong.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter 2022 Compared to the First Quarter 2021

evidence.text: The 9.3% organic revenue growth for the first quarter of 2022 was broad-based across most of our businesses based on solid underlying demand and our ability to produce and ship despite supply chain constraints, input inflation, and unforecasted production interruptions.

factors.summary: Organic growth has shown mixed performance, with evidence of deceleration in certain businesses due to operational challenges and market conditions, yet overall acceleration driven by strong demand and effective supply chain management.

evidence.text: The organic revenue decline was driven by sourced component shortages and logistics headwinds in our marking and coding business, along with reduced activity in our digital textile printing business due to a prolonged recovery in global retail apparel markets.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter 2022 Compared to the First Quarter 2021

evidence.text: The 9.3% organic revenue growth for the first quarter of 2022 was broad-based across most of our businesses based on solid underlying demand and our ability to produce and ship despite supply chain constraints, input inflation, and unforecasted production interruptions.

evidence.text: The 9.3% organic revenue growth for the first quarter of 2022 was broad-based across most of our businesses based on solid underlying demand and our ability to produce and ship despite supply chain constraints, input inflation, and unforecasted production interruptions.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, putting pressure on margins, driven by material, labor, and logistics constraints. However, the company is taking steps to offset these increases through pricing initiatives, productivity actions, and restructuring.

evidence.text: This decrease was primarily driven by lower organic revenues along with material and labor cost inflation, partially offset by pricing initiatives, productivity actions, and the favorable impact from restructuring actions.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter 2022 Compared to the First Quarter 2021

evidence.text: Our pricing initiatives which started in 2021 and into 2022 offset increased material, labor and logistics costs.

factors.summary: End markets are experiencing mixed momentum, with strong growth in biopharma and hygienic markets, as well as in Clean Energy & Fueling and Climate & Sustainability Technologies, but facing challenges in apparel and textile printing due to supply chain issues and logistics constraints.

evidence.text: The organic revenue growth was driven by increased revenue in biopharma and hygienic markets for single-use pumps and connectors used in biopharmaceutical production processes, driven by a strong backlog position entering 2022.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter 2022 Compared to the First Quarter 2021

evidence.text: The Clean Energy & Fueling segment experienced organic revenue decline of 1.1% driven by sourced component shortages and logistics headwinds that impacted shipment volumes in the core marking and coding market and a prolonged recovery in the digital textile printing end market.

factors.summary: Labor costs are a significant challenge, contributing to margin pressure. The company is attempting to manage these costs through restructuring actions, productivity improvements, and pricing initiatives, but labor cost inflation remains a concern.

evidence.text: This decrease was primarily driven by lower organic revenues along with material and labor cost inflation, partially offset by pricing initiatives, productivity actions, and the favorable impact from restructuring actions.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter 2022 Compared to the First Quarter 2021

evidence.text: Higher labor costs, restructuring charges, and acquisition-related amortization expense.

factors.summary: Margins are under significant pressure, driven by cost inflation and other factors. Both gross and segment margins have decreased, with the gross profit margin compressing by 240 basis points and segment margin declining to 18.9% from 20.0% year-over-year.

evidence.text: Segment margin decreased to 18.9% from 20.0% in the prior year comparable quarter.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter 2022 Compared to the First Quarter 2021

evidence.text: Gross profit margin decreased 240 basis points to 36.2%, from the prior year comparable quarter.
Segment margin decreased to 9.0% from 17.1% in the prior year quarter.

factors.summary: The company is implementing cost reduction actions, which are delivering measurable savings but also have offsetting effects on margins due to factors like inflation. While restructuring and other costs are part of these efforts, they may require further actions to achieve desired outcomes.

evidence.text: Cost reduction actions are being implemented, with some offsetting effects on margins.

evidence.text: The increase was primarily driven by conversion on revenue growth, pricing initiatives, and productivity actions, partially offset by material and labor cost inflation, and investments in growth.
evidence.subsection: First Quarter 2022 Compared to the First Quarter 2021

evidence.text: Our pricing initiatives which started in 2021 and into 2022 offset increased material, labor and logistics costs.

factors.summary: The company's capital expenditure outlook is mixed. While there is an expectation of increased capital expenditures for the full year 2022, signaling confidence in future growth, the completion of an acquisition and restructuring activities suggest a cautious investment approach.

evidence.text: We expect full year 2022 capital expenditures to be approximately $200-$220 million.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is showing signs of deterioration. While strong bookings and backlog indicate potential for positive cash flow, the actual free cash flow has decreased significantly due to lower operating cash flow and higher capital expenditures.

evidence.text: Free cash flow for the three months ended March 31, 2022 decreased $172.6 million compared to the prior year period, due to lower operating cash flow primarily as a result of higher compensation payments, increase in inventory, and higher investments in capital expenditures compared to the prior year.
Backlog as of March 31, 2022 was $3.4 billion, an increase from $2.2 billion in the prior year. See definition of bookings and backlog within 'Segment Results of Operations'.

evidence.text: Retail refrigeration revenues increased substantially from the prior year, driven by robust remodel programs with key supermarket customers and strong growth for our natural refrigerant systems in both Europe and the beverage equipment revenues experienced strong growth, driven by continued favorable macro trends in the global beverage industry as producers shift from plastic and glass packaging to aluminum containers for environmental sustainability and merchandising benefits offered by modern aluminum containers.

evidence.text: Dover's entrepreneurial business model encourages, promotes and fosters deep customer engagement and collaboration, which has led to Dover's well-established and valued reputation for providing superior customer service and industry-leading product innovation.

factors.summary: The company is actively engaged in environmental and social initiatives, particularly in sustainable technologies and clean energy solutions. These efforts are driving growth in various sectors, including refrigeration and beverage equipment, as seen through increased revenues and shifts towards more sustainable practices.

evidence.text: Retail refrigeration revenues increased substantially from the prior year, driven by robust remodel programs with key supermarket customers and strong growth for our natural refrigerant systems in both Europe and the beverage equipment revenues experienced strong growth, driven by continued favorable macro trends in the global beverage industry as producers shift from plastic and glass packaging to aluminum containers for environmental sustainability and merchandising benefits offered by modern aluminum containers.

Dover's Climate & Sustainability Technologies segment is a provider of innovative and energy-efficient equipment and systems that serve the commercial refrigeration, heating and cooling and beverage container-making equipment markets.

factors.summary: Dealer/distributor inventory levels are healthy, supporting strong demand signals. Despite a slight decrease in bookings, there was a notable organic growth and positive book-to-bill ratio across all segments, indicating sustained demand.

evidence.text: Overall bookings increased 4.6% as compared to the prior year comparable quarter, reflecting organic growth of 7.7%, acquisition-related growth of 0.4%, and an unfavorable impact from foreign currency translation of 3.5%
evidence.subsection: First Quarter 2022 Compared to the First Quarter 2021

evidence.text: Bookings were $2.3 billion for the three months ended March 31, 2022, a decrease of $80.4 million, or 3.4% compared to the prior year comparable period. Included in this result was organic decline of 4.3%, an unfavorable impact from foreign currency translation of 2.1%, disposition-related decline of 1.4%, and acquisition-related bookings growth of 4.4%. The bookings decline was driven primarily by a difficult comparable quarter in the prior year. Book-to-bill was above 1.00 for all five segments in the quarter, a reflection of strong demand conditions and order intake in most end markets.
